jokes.text: They get you coming, they get you going.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil complaining about $2000 flower bill for Trevor's perpetual care
jokes.punchline: Will's pun on funeral industry profiting from both birth ('coming') and death ('going')
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about funeral industry with efficient wordplay. Classic Will street-smart commentary.

jokes.text: Not any more than Trevor.
jokes.setup: Vivian saying Hilary seemed 'crushed' after Trevor's death
jokes.punchline: Will's deadpan reminder that Trevor was literally crushed in bungee accident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. Perfect economy - four words that completely subvert the setup. Classic Will irreverence with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Whatever that is.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil saying Hilary is 'back to normal'
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic aside questioning what 'normal' means for Hilary
jokes.explanation: Standard character-based humor. Will's typical commentary on family members' quirks. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oh, gosh, how can I put this gently? I can't, he's dead.
jokes.setup: Will pretending to struggle with how to gently tell Hilary something
jokes.punchline: Abandoning gentleness immediately with blunt 'he's dead'
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection. The setup creates expectation of delicacy, then Will's characteristic bluntness delivers the laugh.

jokes.text: Okay, I owe you one.
jokes.setup: Hilary explaining she'll use a TV psychic to channel Trevor
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic acknowledgment that Hilary's 'rational explanation' is actually insane
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat. Will's sarcasm effectively punctures Vivian's attempt to rationalize Hilary's behavior.

jokes.text: Not on the séance, for the séance. I needed a new hat.
jokes.setup: Being questioned about spending $300 on a séance
jokes.punchline: Clarifying the money was for accessories, not the actual service
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment. Her priorities are so warped that she spends more on hat than séance itself. Great character integration.

jokes.text: I don't know. If you ask me, it would be cheaper to just dig him up.
jokes.setup: Discussion of $1000 séance cost
jokes.punchline: Will's macabre suggestion of literal grave-digging as cost-effective alternative
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor. The matter-of-fact delivery of something so morbid is perfectly Will. Great shock value with economic phrasing.

jokes.text: I say, thank God, I'm not a loved one.
jokes.setup: Hilary asking loved ones to attend séance
jokes.punchline: Will expressing relief at not being considered family
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic deflection. Will's attempt to avoid the séance through fake emotional distance works as character moment.

jokes.text: Trevor's bungee accident left everything up in the air.
jokes.setup: Hilary explaining why she needs closure
jokes.punchline: Accidental pun referencing how bungee jumping literally left Trevor 'up in the air'
jokes.explanation: Clever dark pun. Works because Hilary delivers it unconsciously - she doesn't realize the morbid wordplay, making it funnier.

jokes.text: I guess we're just not a close family anymore.
jokes.setup: Family members making excuses to avoid séance
jokes.punchline: Vivian's guilt trip manipulation escalating the emotional stakes
jokes.explanation: Standard guilt trip humor. Sets up the family dynamics but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: But it'll cut into my trick-or-treating time.
jokes.setup: Carlton reluctantly agreeing to attend séance
jokes.punchline: Teenage Carlton still prioritizing Halloween candy collection
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton characterization. His childlike priorities clash humorously with the supernatural situation.

jokes.text: Geoffrey slamming door on trick-or-treaters after saying 'Trick...'
jokes.setup: Geoffrey greeting Halloween children with apparent enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Abruptly slamming door mid-greeting, abandoning pretense
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy moment. Perfect Geoffrey - pretending to care about tradition then immediately abandoning it. Great visual timing.

jokes.text: It's Halloween. It's supposed to be fun for the children. Oh, tell it to Barney.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil criticizing Geoffrey's treatment of trick-or-treaters
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dismissive reference to presumably traumatized child
jokes.explanation: Good Geoffrey deadpan. His casual reference to specific child victim adds perfect specificity to his antisocial behavior.

jokes.text: Halloween, the only night a black man can walk around in this neighborhood with a mask on and not get arrested.
jokes.setup: Will celebrating Halloween night
jokes.punchline: Pointed commentary on racial profiling in wealthy neighborhoods
jokes.explanation: Outstanding social commentary. Classic Fresh Prince - takes serious racial issue and makes it accessible through humor. Perfect Will perspective and highly quotable.

jokes.text: And the Democrats say there's a recession.
jokes.setup: Carlton bragging about Halloween candy haul
jokes.punchline: Using personal candy success to dismiss national economic concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton political naivety. His privileged perspective and childlike reasoning about economics is spot-on characterization.

jokes.text: Why? Because y'all the same height?
jokes.setup: Carlton saying Macaulay Culkin is his idol
jokes.punchline: Will's height joke targeting Carlton's short stature
jokes.explanation: Standard height joke. Predictable Will teasing of Carlton, functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Not to mention, he's one heck of an actor.
jokes.setup: Carlton defending Macaulay Culkin after Will's height joke
jokes.punchline: Carlton's earnest praise of questionable acting ability
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's naive earnestness

jokes.text: Let's go, everyone. I don't wanna keep Trevor waiting. What's the rush? Ain't like he going nowhere.
jokes.setup: Hilary expressing urgency about séance timing
jokes.punchline: Will's logical point that dead people have flexible schedules
jokes.explanation: Good dark logic humor. Will's practical observation about death undermines Hilary's dramatic urgency effectively.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I'm stupid. I'm stupid.
jokes.setup: Will walking into something in the dark
jokes.punchline: His self-deprecating reaction to the mishap
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy moment, gets mild laugh but nothing special

jokes.text: Who would put a deco table on a Persian rug?
jokes.setup: Family commenting on creepy séance location
jokes.punchline: Hilary focusing on interior decorating instead of supernatural atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hilary characterization. Her superficial priorities perfectly contrast with spooky setting. Great misdirection from expected fear reaction.

jokes.text: Stop, don't tell me... You're the Banks family.
jokes.setup: Skorpius claiming psychic powers, dramatically stopping family introduction
jokes.punchline: His 'psychic revelation' is just stating the obvious scheduled appointment
jokes.explanation: Good fake psychic humor. Classic misdirection - builds up psychic moment then reveals mundane explanation.

jokes.text: I want to sit close to the door. That was decent. That was decent. Yeah, well.
jokes.setup: Will expressing desire to sit near exit during séance
jokes.punchline: Carlton's grudging acknowledgment that Will's escape plan makes sense
jokes.explanation: Mild character interaction. Shows Carlton's reluctant respect for Will's practical thinking, but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Seem like somebody made contact with one too many spirits, you know
jokes.setup: Skorpius appearing intoxicated or unstable
jokes.punchline: Will's pun on 'spirits' meaning both ghosts and alcohol
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay. Will's street-smart observation delivered as clever pun works well for his character.

jokes.text: While you're there, see if you can find Marvin Gaye and find out what was really going on.
jokes.setup: Skorpius claiming he can contact spirits
jokes.punchline: Will requesting investigation of famous unsolved celebrity mystery
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference. Will's practical approach to supernatural opportunity shows his street-smart perspective and cultural knowledge.

jokes.text: Seem like some medium ain't paid their electric bill, huh?
jokes.setup: Lights going out during séance for dramatic effect
jokes.punchline: Will's mundane explanation for supernatural theatrics
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of supernatural atmosphere. Will's practical perspective cuts through the drama effectively.

jokes.text: Oh, they wish me to tell you: 'Shut your pie hole.'
jokes.setup: Skorpius claiming to channel spirits with important message
jokes.punchline: The profound spiritual communication is just telling Will to be quiet
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of mystical expectations. The contrast between spiritual setup and crude punchline works well.

jokes.text: I know his vibration.
jokes.setup: Skorpius describing sensing Trevor's presence
jokes.punchline: Hilary's inappropriately intimate knowledge claim about deceased fiancé
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Hilary's oversharing about intimate relationship details creates appropriate awkwardness.

jokes.text: I thought they was boring when he was alive.
jokes.setup: Hilary and 'Trevor' having romantic supernatural conversation
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment that death hasn't improved Trevor's personality
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery. Will's observation that supernatural romance is still tedious is perfectly timed and in-character.

jokes.text: Do you know where I left my black suede purse?
jokes.setup: Hilary having chance to ask deceased fiancé anything
jokes.punchline: Her burning question is about lost accessories
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment. The ultimate shallow question for profound supernatural opportunity. Brilliant character consistency and absurd priorities.

jokes.text: Come on, man, I could be sucking on some honey neck bone right now.
jokes.setup: Séance dragging on with boring conversation
jokes.punchline: Will's specific, appetizing alternative to supernatural tedium
jokes.explanation: Good Will characterization. The specific food reference and street vernacular contrast nicely with supernatural setting.

jokes.text: Oh, like you ever had it.
jokes.setup: Skorpius claiming he's 'losing' his psychic connection
jokes.punchline: Will's implication that Skorpius never had real powers
jokes.explanation: Solid skeptical quip. Will's dismissal of fake psychic is economical and in-character.

jokes.text: Trevor has left the building.
jokes.setup: Séance ending with Trevor's spirit departing
jokes.punchline: Elvis reference applied to dead fiancé's supernatural exit
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference. Functional but predictable application of famous Elvis phrase to supernatural context.

jokes.text: And miss Saved By The Bell? I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Skorpius suggesting another séance tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Will's TV priorities trumping supernatural activities
jokes.explanation: Good teen character moment. Will's pop culture priorities show his age and normal teen concerns amid supernatural drama.

jokes.text: right between the exorcism and step class.
jokes.setup: Skorpius checking his schedule for another séance
jokes.punchline: Casual pairing of supernatural services with mundane fitness class
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist juxtaposition. The matter-of-fact scheduling of supernatural and mundane activities works well.

jokes.text: Listen, Scrupulous.
jokes.setup: Will addressing the fake psychic Skorpius
jokes.punchline: Deliberately mispronouncing name as 'Scrupulous' (having moral integrity)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay. Will's 'mispronunciation' is actually pointed commentary on Skorpius's lack of ethics. Smart verbal joke.

jokes.text: Nobody calls me the F-word.
jokes.setup: Will calling Skorpius a 'fake'
jokes.punchline: Skorpius treating 'fake' as profane insult equivalent to actual f-word
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection. Audience expects actual profanity reference, but it's just Skorpius being overly sensitive about professional reputation.

jokes.text: I placed a curse on you.
jokes.setup: Skorpius being exposed as fraud
jokes.punchline: Escalating to dramatic curse pronouncement as final manipulation
jokes.explanation: Sets up episode's main plot device. Functional escalation but not particularly funny in isolation.

jokes.text: How do you think I ended up here?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking if people can really be cursed
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's implication that his butler position is result of curse
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey moment. Perfect economy - five words that explain his entire attitude toward his job. Great deadpan character work.

jokes.text: So if I hook up with some babe tonight, I promise to practice safe hex.
jokes.setup: Will dismissing curse concerns before going to party
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'safe sex' adapted to supernatural context
jokes.explanation: Decent pun. Will's casual approach to both supernatural and sexual matters fits character, though joke is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Carlton, what's that hideous thing growing out of your neck? What, what? Where, where? Hold on, hold on, hold on. Never mind. It's just your head.
jokes.setup: Will pretending to see something horrible on Carlton's neck
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's just insulting Carlton's head/face
jokes.explanation: Standard insult joke. Well-structured misdirection but relies on basic 'your face' humor. Effective for Will-Carlton dynamic.

jokes.text: Carlton, what the hell happened to your hair? Oh, no, you don't. I'm not falling for that again. Oh, my hair. I look like a snow cone.
jokes.setup: Carlton's hair turned white/blonde, Will commenting on it
jokes.punchline: Carlton's panic and 'snow cone' comparison to his appearance
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback. Carlton's initial skepticism then horror creates nice comedy arc. 'Snow cone' is vivid, specific description.

jokes.text: For a moment there, I thought you'd found those Polaroids of me in a tutu. New Year's Eve. A fellow gets crazy.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey relieved about Ashley's tennis accident vs. other concern
jokes.punchline: Revealing embarrassing cross-dressing photos with casual explanation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey moment. Perfect character work - his casual revelation of bizarre secret with matter-of-fact justification is hilarious.

jokes.text: until we all end up on Unsolved Mysteries.
jokes.setup: Carlton warning about curse picking family off one by one
jokes.punchline: Specific pop culture reference to TV show about unexplained deaths
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference. Carlton's specific TV show reference adds humor to his paranoid predictions.

jokes.text: The hex has gone international.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey being deported by immigration
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dramatic assessment of curse expanding globally
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke. Carlton's overly dramatic interpretation of immigration issue fits his paranoid character arc.

jokes.text: What I need is a judge who can be bought off, like you.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil offering legal help to Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey casually accepting corruption charges as true
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy. Geoffrey's casual acceptance of corruption accusations while asking for corrupt help is perfectly cynical.

jokes.text: To think, all these years, I never appreciated you.
jokes.setup: Hilary announcing engagement to Jazz
jokes.punchline: Her 'romantic' confession about never appreciating Jazz before
jokes.explanation: Standard Hilary superficiality. Her backhanded romantic declaration fits character but isn't particularly strong comedy.

jokes.text: I'll be in the kitchen throwing up.
jokes.setup: Jazz and Hilary's romantic display
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's matter-of-fact announcement of his disgusted response
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey deadpan. Perfect economy and character voice - his polite announcement of crude response is perfectly British butler.

jokes.text: We're gonna name our first son after you, Daddy. By the way, I thought we could get started tonight.
jokes.setup: Jazz trying to ingratiate himself with Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggesting sexual activity with Hilary in front of her father
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation. Jazz's social obliviousness creates appropriate family awkwardness, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: Your little Willie. You know, the adorable, charismatic, sometimes troublesome yet overall wonderful young man you've grown to love.
jokes.setup: Will pleading not to be thrown out
jokes.punchline: His overly elaborate self-description and shameless self-promotion
jokes.explanation: Good Will desperation moment. His shameless self-promotion and manipulation attempt fits character during crisis.

jokes.text: Listen, take anything you want. Just don't hurt me.
jokes.setup: Will arriving at Skorpius's house to apologize
jokes.punchline: Skorpius thinking Will is there to rob him
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection. The robbery assumption creates brief comedy but isn't particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: Will waking up from nightmare - entire hex sequence was a dream
jokes.setup: All the supernatural disasters and family conflicts
jokes.punchline: Revealing entire hex storyline was Will's nightmare
jokes.explanation: Weak 'it was all a dream' resolution. Classic lazy writing trope that undermines everything preceding it. Cheap narrative reset.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey, listen. How about those Lakers, huh?
jokes.setup: The exact same conversation beginning to repeat
jokes.punchline: Will desperately tries to change the subject to prevent repetition
jokes.explanation: Decent attempt to salvage the dream ending by showing Will's awareness of the loop

jokes.text: Ronnie, Bobby, Ricky, Mike?
jokes.setup: Hilary asking who she's going out with tonight
jokes.punchline: Will hoping for New Edition members instead of Trevor
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference. Will's hope for R&B group members shows his musical knowledge and desperate optimism to avoid supernatural repetition.
